title: Bachelor of Technology in Embedded Systems
content: The B.Tech in Embedded Systems program is a specialized undergraduate engineering degree that equips students with the skills and knowledge required to design, develop, and optimize embedded hardware and software systems. Embedded systems are at the heart of modern technology, driving applications in areas such as the Internet of Things (IoT), smart appliances, medical devices, automotive electronics, robotics, consumer electronics, industrial automation, and more. Minimum 50% marks in Physics, Mathematics and Chemistry/Computer Science/Information Technology/Informatics Practices/Electronics/ 01 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and embedded system engineering to the solution of complex real-world problems. 02 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems in embedded system design using principles of electronics, computing, and systems engineering. 03 Design/Development of Solutions: Design system-level embedded solutions, components, or processes that meet specified requirements with appropriate consideration for public health, safety, societal, and environmental concerns. 04 Investigation: Conduct investigations of complex embedded problems using research-based knowledge and methods including design of experiments, simulation, analysis, and interpretation of data to draw valid conclusions. 05 Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate embedded development platforms, simulation tools, modeling frameworks, and modern engineering software to complex engineering activities, recognizing their limitations. 06 The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, legal, health, safety, and cultural issues relevant to the embedded engineering practice. 07 Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of embedded systems on environmental sustainability and demonstrate the ability to design energy-efficient and green embedded solutions. 08 Ethics: Apply ethical principles, uphold professional ethics, responsibilities, and norms of embedded and electronics engineering practice. 09 Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams and multidisciplinary settings, especially in embedded product development environments. 10 Communication: Communicate effectively on complex embedded engineering activities with the engineering community and society through reports, design documentation, presentations, and technical discussions. 11 Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge of project management and financial principles, and apply these to embedded system projects in multidisciplinary teams and organizational settings. 01 Graduates shall apply core knowledge of embedded systems, electronics, and computing to design and develop efficient and reliable embedded solutions for real-world applications. 02 Graduates shall pursue successful careers or higher education in embedded systems, IoT, automation, or related fields, while contributing to technological innovation and interdisciplinary research. 03 Graduates shall demonstrate professionalism, ethical responsibility, and a commitment to lifelong learning, while contributing to society through leadership, teamwork, and entrepreneurial initiatives. 01 Embedded Hardware and Software Proficiency: Graduates will be able to design, program, and debug embedded systems using microcontrollers, real-time operating systems (RTOS), and industry-standard development tools and languages. 02 System Integration/Application Development and Optimization: Graduates will demonstrate the ability to integrate sensors, actuators, communication interfaces, and embedded platforms for building efficient solutions/optimization in domains like IoT, industrial automation, and consumer electronics. Minimum 50% marks in Physics, Mathematics and Chemistry/Computer Science/Information Technology/Informatics Practices/Electronics/
